_comment: Source slug + priority registry consumed by backend/scripts/ingest_all.py.
Each top-level key is a registered source name (must match the string
passed to register_source(...) inside backend/app/services/ingestion).
Each entry is an object: {priority: int, slugs: [string]}.
The legacy flat shape  {source: [slug, ...]}  is still accepted by
_load_config — priority defaults to 999 (lowest) for those entries.
Priority is consumed by PR 3's merge step: when N sources contribute
the same posting_key, the lowest-numbered priority wins each field
(freshness breaks ties). PR 2 only writes posting_key + sightings;
no merge happens yet, so priority is shadow data until PR 3 ships.
Priority order rationale (see docs/level-up/adrs/0006-cached-jobs-dedup.md):
ATS-native feeds (1-4) carry richer structured fields than
aggregators (5-6). Within ATS, ashby > greenhouse > lever > workable
by API completeness (compensation/team metadata coverage).
ATS sources (greenhouse/lever/ashby/workable/smartrecruiters) take
company-board slugs. WeWorkRemotely takes RSS category slugs because
it is an aggregator with no per-company board.
Verify a slug before adding it: ATS migrations happen monthly. Run
python -m backend.scripts.audit_sources --source <name> --ids <slug>
first; only commit slugs that returned >0 jobs.
Lines starting with `_` are ignored by the orchestrator.
